}}This article details the [[Bradford Bulls]] rugby league football club's 2017 season. This is the Bulls' 3rd season in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]]. They csme into this season as [[Championship Shield]] winners after defeating the [[Sheffield Eagles]] 27-16 in the Championship Shield Final.

==Season review==

===August 2016===

It was announced that French international [[Jean-Philippe Baile]] left the Bulls by mutual consent after playing just one game in the 2016 season.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} During the same week, newly formed [[RFL League 1|League 1]] side [[Toronto Wolfpack]] signed Bulls {{rlp|pr}} [[Daniel Fleming (rugby league)|Dan Fleming]] on a one-year contract.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Tongan winger [[Etu Uaisele]] also left Bradford after being sent on loan to [[Dewsbury Rams]], he returned home to [[Australia]] following his release.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Fullback [[Richie Mathers]] announced his retirement shortly after the defeat by Dewsbury Rams.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} [[Super League]] side [[Warrington Wolves]] signed Bradford centre and former player [[Matty Blythe]] on a one-year deal.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Captain [[Adrian Purtell]] announced that he would join the [[London Broncos]] on a two-year deal, shortly after second rower [[Jay Pitts]] joined the departing Purtell to join London.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Veteran prop forward [[Rhys Lovegrove]] announced his retirement from the sport after suffering from a recurring head injury, he joined the backroom staff.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/14706862.retiring-bulls-star-lovegrove-to-take-up-coaching-role-at-odsal/|title=Retiring Bulls star Lovegrove to take up coaching role at Odsal|first=Ross|last=Heppenstall|work=[[Telegraph & Argus]]|date=26 August 2016|access-date=2 November 2024}}</ref> The first signings for the 2017 season came in the form of a London trio: winger [[Ilies Macani]], centre [[Alex Foster (rugby league)|Alex Foster]] and prop forward [[Jon Magrin]], all on two-year deals.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/14704013.bradford-bulls-boss-smith-boosted-by-broncos-trio/|title=Bradford Bulls boss Smith boosted by Broncos trio|first=Ross|last=Heppenstall|work=[[Telegraph & Argus]]|date=24 August 2016|access-date=2 November 2024}}</ref> Towards the end of the month the Bulls announced that former academy graduate and club legend [[Leon Pryce]] would be returning on a one-year deal in order to finish his career at the Bradford Bulls.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}}

===September 2016===

[[Lucas Walshaw]] made his loan deal at [[Dewsbury Rams]] into a permanent one by signing a two-year contract.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] centre/second row [[Jason Walton]] became the 5th signing for the 2017 season by putting pen to paper on a two-year deal at the Bulls.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Bradford born prop forward [[Ross Peltier]] signed a two-year deal with the Bulls from local side [[Keighley Cougars]].{{citation needed|date=March 2017}}

===October 2016===

Long serving academy loose forward [[Danny Addy]] and New Zealand prop forward Mitch Clark both signed one-year deals at [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Due to Hull KR's relegation, both of their contracts were voided and had the option to re-sign for the Bulls; however, both decided to honor their contracts with the Hull side.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Ex-Bradford player [[Phil Joseph]] signed a two-year deal to rejoin the club after a lack of game time at [[Salford Red Devils]], shortly after the Bulls announced the signing of Australian loose forward [[Lachlan Burr]] from the [[Gold Coast Titans]] on a one-year deal.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Bradford were due to sign [[Manly Sea Eagles]] forward [[Blake Leary]], but negotiations fell through at the vital point of talks.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}}

===November 2016===

Second-row forward [[Kurt Haggerty]] retired from playing rugby league to further his coaching career, he signed on the backroom staff for new Canadian side [[Toronto Wolfpack]].{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Second rower [[Tom Olbison]] also signed a one-year extension to his contract.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/14845813.olbison-delighted-to-finally-commit-to-bradford-bulls/|title=Olbison delighted to finally commit to Bradford Bulls|first=Ross|last=Heppenstall|work=[[Telegraph & Argus]]|date=5 November 2016|access-date=2 November 2024}}</ref> Prop forward [[Steve Crossley]] announced that he had signed for Toronto for the upcoming season.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} [[Huddersfield Giants]] announced the signing of prop [[Paul Clough]] from the Bulls. In addition to this stand off, [[Lee Gaskell]] signed a four-year deal with the Giants.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} During this month, owner Marc Green placed the club in administration for the third time in four years due to outstanding tax owed to [[HMRC]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/nov/14/bradford-bulls-placed-into-administration-rugby-league|title=Bradford Bulls have been placed into administration|work=[[The Guardian]]|date=14 November 2016|access-date=2 November 2024}}</ref> Meanwhile, Huddersfield announced the signing of [[Dale Ferguson (rugby league)|Dale Ferguson]] from the Bulls on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/38130574|title=Dale Ferguson: Bradford Bulls second-rower rejoins Huddersfield Giants|work=[[BBC Sport]]|date=28 November 2016|access-date=2 November 2024}}</ref>

===December 2016===

Due to the club being in administration, no signings were made in December.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} According to the administrator there were a number of bids for the club include a couple from multi-millionaires hoping to invest in [[Odsal Stadium]] and its surrounding lands.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} However, none of these were successful.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}}

===January 2017===

The administrators announced that two last minute bids were unsuccessful and therefore the club was liquidated.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Following this news, winger [[James Clare (rugby league)|James Clare]] signed for [[Leigh Centurions]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/38529145|title=James Clare: Leigh Centurions sign Bradford Bulls winger|work=[[BBC Sport]]|date=6 January 2017|access-date=2 November 2024}}</ref> This sparked an exodus at Odsal as players became free agents.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Academy products [[Tom Olbison]], [[Alex Mellor]] and [[Adam O'Brien]] all signed with [[Super League]] clubs as Olbison joined [[Widnes Vikings]] with Mellor and O'Brien joining the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on long term deals.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Top try scorer [[Kris Welham]] attracted numerous offers before settling on [[Salford Red Devils]] and second rower [[Jason Walton]] signed with [[RFL Championship]] side [[Featherstone Rovers]].{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Superstar fullback [[Kieren Moss]] and prop [[Ben Kavanagh]] also signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} The [[Rugby Football League]] (RFL) accepted a bid to found a new club in Bradford.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} It was announced that Andrew Chalmers and a former [[Wigan Warriors]] coach, [[Graham Lowe]], were the successful new owners.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} They immediately signed veteran halfback [[Leon Pryce]] and released coach [[Rohan Smith]].{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} In addition to this, they declined to offer Aussie halfback [[Dane Chisholm]] a contract with the new club.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} During the owners press conference they revealed that [[Manly Sea Eagles]] legend [[Geoff Toovey]] would become the Bulls' new coach for the 2017 season. Leon Pryce was revealed as the captain.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Australian second rower [[Lachlan Burr]] signed for Leigh Centurions.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/38682199|title=Lachlan Burr: Leigh Centurions sign Bradford Bulls forward|work=[[BBC Sport]]|date=19 January 2017|access-date=2 November 2024}}</ref> The Bulls re-signed the rest of the off contract players.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} With a makeshift side the Bulls lost 10-28 to a strong [[Huddersfield Giants]] side with [[Joe Keyes (rugby league)|Joe Keyes]] and [[Ross Oakes]] scoring for Bradford, Keyes kicked a conversion.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Second-row forward [[Colton Roche]] put pen to paper on a deal to join the Bulls for the 2017 season from [[York City Knights]].{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} In the final pre-season match, Bradford narrowly lost 24-25 to [[RFL League 1|League 1]] side [[Keighley Cougars]]. New signing Roche scored the opening try before young hooker Joe Lumb scored.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Oakes carried on from the previous week and scored whilst [[Iliess Macani]] also went over for a try, Keyes kicked three goals and [[Oscar Thomas]] kicked a conversion. Squad numbers were released at the end of the month, with Pryce receiving the number 6 shirt.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}}

===February 2017===

Reinforces were made to the squad as [[Leeds Rhinos]] loaned out halfback [[Jordan Lilley]], second row [[Josh Jordan-Roberts]], hooker [[Sam Hallas]] and prop Mikolaj Oledzki on a one-month loan to the Bulls ahead of the opening game.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} The opening game of the season saw the Bulls lose 24-54 to [[RFL Championship]] favourites [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. The Bulls took an early lead through young prop [[Liam Kirk]] before Hull KR scored a few tries to take the lead. Academy graduate [[Ross Oakes]] also scored before fan favourite [[Ethan Ryan]] raced 80m to score an interception try ten minutes from full-time. [[Iliess Macani]] scored a consolation try after a [[Jordan Lilley]] intercept whilst fullback [[Oscar Thomas]] kicked four conversions.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Following the defeat the Bulls announced the capture of young Samoan [[rugby sevens]] star [[Phoenix Hunapo-Nofoa]] on a one-year deal.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} The first home game of the season saw 4,051 people watch the Bulls narrowly lose 14-22 to the [[Rochdale Hornets]]. Youngster [[James Bentley (rugby league)|James Bentley]] and Thomas both scored tries whilst Thomas kicked 3 goals in the defeat.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} After being out powered in the pack the Bulls announced the signing of French international, second row forward [[Kevin Larroyer]] on a one-month loan deal from [[Castleford Tigers]].{{citation needed|date=March 2017}} Bradford added more experience signing ex-international [[Scott Moore (rugby league)|Scott Moore]] on a 4-game trial and also utility back [[Lee Smith (rugby)|Lee Smith]] on a two-year deal.{{citation needed|date=March 2017}}
